preregistration.method: Identical to fork-B tprime_experiment.py: leave-one-out over each frozen multi-site fix case; B0 = frozen top-100 lexical cosine pool in lexical order; T' = SAME frozen pool re-ranked by repair applicability (lexical cosine tie-break). No tuning. bootstrap=2000, seed=20260704, macro over CASES.
preregistration.note: 12 repos committed before any T'/B0 computation (selection = candidate-count ranking + broad coverage of the 17 mined repos). Slightly exceeds the 6-10 guideline to add power and avoid post-hoc repo selection.
preregistration.null_rule: label NULL/precision-mode-only, OR no primary-metric CI excludes 0 at the +0.05 bar.
preregistration.power_floor: Require >=40 runtime-only cases; else UNDERPOWERED (report N + repos needed).
preregistration.question: Does mined repair-intent reranking (T') beat a stranger's own lexical grep top-100 pool (B0) on the stranger's OWN multi-site fix siblings? WIN refutes Fable-C's intra-org 'category error' and makes mining product-shaped; NULL confines mining to intra-org.
preregistration.runtime_only_filter: Drop a case iff ALL of its sibling sites are test files (test-ONLY). Validated to reproduce fork-B 82->47 exactly (symdiff=0). test-file predicate: dir in {tests,test,__tests__,testing} OR basename test_*/ *_test.py / conftest.py / *.test.* / *.spec.*
preregistration.win_rule_ci_anchor: Declare WIN only if verdict label=='practical-win' AND at least one of {recall@10, nDCG@10} has delta>=+0.05 with bootstrap 95% CI lower bound > 0 (the fork-B bar as stated in the 1c task).
preregistration.win_rule_primary: Reuse the UNCHANGED tprime verdict_from_results (thresholds a OR b OR c): (a) nDCG@10 delta>=0.03 AND recall@10 delta>=-0.02; (b) nDCG@10>=-0.01 AND recall@10>=-0.02 AND hard-negative@10 cut fraction>=0.30; (c) P@3 delta>=0.05 AND recall tied-or-better.

cases.subject: Fix Decimal() and __ne__() errors.
Display collect progress only when in a terminal
Make monkeypatch calls O(1)
Implement ALLOW_BYTES doctest option
Fix monkeypatch.setenv with string and raising=False.

fix issue616 - conftest visibility fixes.  This is achieved by refactoring how nodeid's are constructed.  They now are always relative to the "common rootdir" of a test run which is determined by finding a common ancestor of all testrun arguments.
Show user assertion messages and instrospection together
simplify reset/stop_capturing and fix capturing wrt to capturing simple os.write() calls
- fix issue181: --pdb now also works on collect errors.  This was   implemented by a slight internal refactoring and the introduction   of a new hook ``pytest_exception_interact`` hook.
Fixed issue #306: Keywords and markers are now matched in a defined way. Also applied some pep8 formatting while fixing.
rename `get_fixture` to `getfixture` to better match the current API style
fix issue148 - recognize @unittest.skip on classes, avoid setup/teardown
refine parsefactories interface, fix two_classes test originally reported by Alex Okrushko, also add a few more tests to make sure autouse-fixtures are properly distinguished
rename a number of internal and externally visible variables to use the fixture name rather than funcargs.  Introduce .funcargnames compatibility attribute for backward compat.
fix skip/xfail confusion, reported and discussed on http://stackoverflow.com/questions/11105828/in-py-test-when-i-explicitly-skip-a-test-that-is-marked-as-xfail-how-can-i-get
fix #59: provide better Jenkins stdout and stderr sections
streamline some hook docs and option handling, remove cruft bits, fix doc links
introduce py.builtin._sysex as alias for the special exceptions, fixes #115
introduce a new --junitprefix option to influence xml reporting. also internally avoid some redundant code.
fix issue104 properly xml-escape names in junitxml files
fix issue96 - make capturing more resilient against KeyboardInterrupt

changes: +""" resume capturing with original temp files. """
+""" resume capturing with original temp files. """

cases.subject: fix issue78 - strike superflous "import sys" that cause unbound local var errors.
* make Gateway interface more asymetric: remote_* methods   and  cleanup/atexit handling now live exclusively with the "InitiatingGateway"
* fix some syntax and 3k issues for py/path and py/process, tests only partially working * have py.process.cmdexec return unicode/text (for now) * rename py.builtin.basestring to _basestring
* deprecate py.magic.invoke/revoke in favour of   the new py.code.patch_builtins, py.code.unpatch_builtins
move localpath implementation to a single file, simplify unix/posix difference and fix a bit

cases.subject: fixing docs, adding newest ez_setup.py, fixing a few win32 failures, fixing iocapture plugin
* fix too-many-open files issue with fd based io-capturing * workaround 2.6 issue with pickling of objects from global conftest.py files
apply and generalize patch from Ronny regarding dumb terminals, add doc note
[svn r63014] streamlining multichannel interface, fixing test work with -n 3
[svn r57756] * create new contrib directory * move py/green to contrib/pygreen,   fix tests and code to pass

[svn r38439] Fixed a list of things suggested by hpk: changed method order in class pages, changed page titles, added links to the api and source index from the nav bar (also in py/doc html), changed function views, made it possible to remove an item from the navigation, changed header 'properties' to 'class attributes and properties', removed duplicate stack traces (in a somewhat unsatisfying way, needs revisiting later I think).

[svn r37913] Some more cleanups in HTML generation, fixed support for docstrings in namespaces, in order to do this I had to change the way objects are retrieved: instead of getting them from the DSA I now walk the package tree, small change in apigen.py: to allow re-using the get_documentable_items function I split it up in a generic and a specific part.
Pass args from `__init__` to `__attrs_pre_init__` if requested (#1187)
Speedup `_setattr` usage and fix slight performance regressions (#991)
Optimize the case of on_setattr=validate & no validators (#817)
Treat frozensets like sets in asdict/astuple (#704)
